ucs.title: [LLM] Famous Chollima 'Contagious Interview' — node.exe → python chain dropping BeaverTail/InvisibleFerret with browser-credential and crypto-wall
ucs.description: Hunts the DPRK Famous Chollima (CrowdStrike) / UNC5342 (Mandiant) developer-targeting campaign where a trojanized npm/GitHub 'coding challenge' executes via Node, then spawns a Python implant (InvisibleFerret) that reads Chromium 'Login Data' SQLite stores and crypto-wallet extension folders. The node→python pivot from a developer's Code/Terminal session combined with Login Data file access is highly specific to this actor.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=t count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.process_name IN ("node.exe","node") AND Processes.parent_process_name IN ("Code.exe","Cursor.exe","cmd.exe","powershell.exe","WindowsTerminal.exe","bash","zsh","Terminal") AND (Processes.process="*main.js*" OR Processes.process="*error.js*" OR Processes.process="*server.js*" OR Processes.process="*tailwind.config*" OR Processes.process="*helper.js*") by Processes.dest Processes.user Processes.process Processes.parent_process_name | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | rename process as node_cmd | join type=inner dest [ | tstats summariesonly=t count as py_count earliest(_time) as py_time from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.process_name IN ("python.exe","python3","pythonw.exe","python") AND Processes.parent_process_name IN ("node.exe","node","cmd.exe","powershell.exe") by Processes.dest Processes.process | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | rename process as py_cmd ] | where py_time >= firstTime AND (py_time - lastTime) < 1800 | join type=inner dest [ | tstats summariesonly=t count as wallet_or_login_hits from datamodel=Endpoint.Filesystem where (Filesystem.file_path="*Login Data*" OR Filesystem.file_path="*Local Extension Settings*nkbihfbeogaeaoehlefnkodbefgpgknn*" OR Filesystem.file_path="*Local Extension Settings*ejbalbakoplchlghecdalmeeeajnimhm*" OR Filesystem.file_path="*solana*id.json*" OR Filesystem.file_path="*Exodus*exodus.wallet*") AND Filesystem.process_name IN ("node.exe","node","python.exe","python3","pythonw.exe") by Filesystem.dest Filesystem.process_name Filesystem.file_path | `drop_dm_object_name(Filesystem)` ] | table firstTime lastTime dest user parent_process_name node_cmd py_cmd file_path wallet_or_login_hits | `security_content_ctime(firstTime)` | `security_content_ctime(lastTime)`
ucs.kql:
let window = 30m;
let devSessions = DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(14d)
| where FileName in~ ("node.exe","node")
| where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ ("Code.exe","Cursor.exe","cmd.exe","powershell.exe","WindowsTerminal.exe","bash","zsh","Terminal")
| where ProcessCommandLine has_any ("main.js","error.js","server.js","tailwind.config","helper.js","dist/index")
| project NodeTime=Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, AccountName, NodePid=ProcessId, NodeCmd=ProcessCommandLine;
let pySpawn = DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(14d)
| where FileName in~ ("python.exe","python3","pythonw.exe","python")
| where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ ("node.exe","node","cmd.exe","powershell.exe")
| project PyTime=Timestamp, DeviceId, PyPid=ProcessId, PyCmd=ProcessCommandLine, PyParent=InitiatingProcessFileName;
let credAccess = DeviceFileEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(14d)
| where FolderPath has_any ("\\Google\\Chrome\\User Data\\","\\Microsoft\\Edge\\User Data\\","\\BraveSoftware\\","/Library/Application Support/Google/Chrome/","Local Extension Settings\\nkbihfbeogaeaoehlefnkodbefgpgknn","Local Extension Settings\\ejbalbakoplchlghecdalmeeeajnimhm","Exodus\\exodus.wallet",".config/solana")
| where FileName in~ ("Login Data","Cookies","Web Data","id.json","exodus.wallet") or FolderPath has "Local Extension Settings"
| where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ ("node.exe","node","python.exe","python3","pythonw.exe")
| project FileTime=Timestamp, DeviceId, ToucherPid=InitiatingProcessId, ToucherImg=InitiatingProcessFileName, FilePath=strcat(FolderPath,FileName);
devSessions
| join kind=inner pySpawn on DeviceId
| where PyTime between (NodeTime .. (NodeTime + window))
| join kind=inner credAccess on DeviceId
| where FileTime between (NodeTime .. (NodeTime + 2h))
| summarize FilesTouched=make_set(FilePath,25), NodeCmd=any(NodeCmd), PyCmd=any(PyCmd), PyParent=any(PyParent) by DeviceId, DeviceName, AccountName, bin(NodeTime, 1h)
| where array_length(FilesTouched) >= 1
ucs.rationale: Famous Chollima's 'Contagious Interview' campaign (BeaverTail JS stealer → InvisibleFerret Python RAT) is the only widely reported actor whose canonical chain is: developer launches a 'coding test' under Code/Cursor/Terminal → node.exe runs a benign-looking JS file → that node process spawns python.exe which immediately reaches into Chromium Login Data and Solana/MetaMask/Phantom/Exodus wallet art

ucs.title: [LLM] Famous Chollima DPRK IT-worker fraud — multi-RMM stack-up on developer endpoint with Astrill/residential-proxy SaaS sign-ins
ucs.description: Hunts the second Famous Chollima TTP set (DPRK 'Wagemole' / IT-worker scheme): a freshly-onboarded remote engineer's corporate device installs multiple residential RMM/remote-control tools (AnyDesk, Chrome Remote Desktop, RustDesk, TeamViewer) in a tight window while the same identity's GitHub/Slack/Jira/M365 sign-ins originate from Astrill VPN or known DPRK-laundering residential-proxy ASNs.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=t values(Processes.process_name) as rmm_tools dc(Processes.process_name) as rmm_count min(_time) as firstInstall max(_time) as lastInstall from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.process_name IN ("AnyDesk.exe","remoting_host.exe","chromeremotedesktophost.exe","rustdesk.exe","TeamViewer.exe","TeamViewer_Service.exe","ScreenConnect.ClientService.exe","AteraAgent.exe","splashtop*.exe") by Processes.dest Processes.user span=7d | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | where rmm_count >= 2 | join type=inner user [ | tstats summariesonly=t values(Authentication.src) as src_ips values(Authentication.app) as apps count from datamodel=Authentication where Authentication.action=success AND Authentication.app IN ("github","slack","jira","office365","azuread","okta","gsuite") by Authentication.user Authentication.src_country | `drop_dm_object_name(Authentication)` | stats values(src_country) as countries values(src_ips) as src_ips values(apps) as apps by user | where mvcount(countries) >= 2 OR match(mvjoin(countries,","),"(?i)KP|RU|CN|HK|LA|MY") ] | table firstInstall lastInstall dest user rmm_tools rmm_count countries src_ips apps | `security_content_ctime(firstInstall)`
ucs.kql:
let lookback = 14d;
let rmmStack = DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(lookback)
| where FileName in~ ("AnyDesk.exe","remoting_host.exe","chromeremotedesktophost.exe","rustdesk.exe","TeamViewer.exe","TeamViewer_Service.exe","ScreenConnect.ClientService.exe","AteraAgent.exe","splashtop.exe","SRService.exe")
  and ActionType == "ProcessCreated"
| summarize RmmTools=make_set(FileName), FirstSeen=min(Timestamp), LastSeen=max(Timestamp) by DeviceId, DeviceName, AccountUpn=tostring(AccountUpn)
| where array_length(RmmTools) >= 2 and (LastSeen - FirstSeen) < 7d;
let riskySignIns = AADSignInEventsBeta
| where Timestamp > ago(lookback)
| where ErrorCode == 0
| where Application has_any ("GitHub","Slack","Atlassian","Office 365","Microsoft 365","Azure")
| extend isProxy = tostring(parse_json(tostring(NetworkLocationDetails))) has_any ("trustedNamedLocation","compliantNetwork") == false
| summarize Countries=make_set(Country), ASNs=make_set(NetworkLocationDetails), IPs=make_set(IPAddress), Apps=make_set(Application), SignIns=count() by AccountUpn
| where array_length(Countries) >= 2
     or Countries has_any ("KP","RU","CN","HK","LA","MY")
     or tostring(ASNs) has_any ("Astrill","AS9009","AS59729","M247","residential","Datacamp");
rmmStack
| join kind=inner riskySignIns on AccountUpn
| project FirstSeen, LastSeen, DeviceName, AccountUpn, RmmTools, Countries, Apps, IPs, SignIns
| order by FirstSeen desc
ucs.rationale: The DPRK IT-worker fraud (Famous Chollima / Wagemole, also tracked by Mandiant as UNC5267) has a uniquely operational fingerprint: the actor needs simultaneous remote control (because the laptop is physically in a US laptop-farm but the operator is in DPRK/RU/CN), so they layer 2-3 RMM tools on day one and front identity logins through Astrill VPN or rented residential proxies. Legitimate engineer
